rationales.hedonism: He "pulled two all-nighters" and says his "body is wrecked," showing self-denial and disregard for present comfort.
rationales.achievement: He drove himself to "get the chapter revision done" and submitted it at "4am," showing strong effort toward academic success.
rationales.benevolence: He "snapped" at Dina when she was checking on him and then admits he'd been "neglecting everything that wasn't the dissertation," which harmed a close relationship.

rationales.self_direction: He is drawn to the "completely hands-off" option and says being "boxed in" means he is "about to betray something important" about how he wants to live, prioritizing autonomy over outside control.
rationales.security: He is resisting the investor with "more capital" because oversight makes his "chest tighten," showing he is willing to give up a safer, better-funded option to preserve independence.

rationales.hedonism: After signing the papers, he "celebrated with Layla at that new Persian restaurant" and says it "felt right," emphasizing present enjoyment and pleasure.

rationales.self_direction: He rejected the placement because he wants to be "building something from scratch" and insists "My project, my call," clearly prioritizing authorship over external direction.
rationales.security: He turned down a placement whose name on his CV "would open doors," choosing a less secure path over a safer prestige boost.
rationales.conformity: He resists family and social pressure to take the prestigious option, saying he does not need to justify his choice and will avoid the expected argument until he has results.

rationales.self_direction: He insists on "building my own pipeline" and admits he keeps refusing help because he needs it to be "entirely mine," showing a strong drive for independence.
rationales.achievement: He is "already behind on coursework," stayed up until four debugging, and still has not started the fellowship statement despite the deadline, so his current choices are undermining competent performance.
rationales.conformity: He rejects collaborative expectations, saying he refused Nina's help immediately and cannot honestly present himself as "a team player," which conflicts with fitting expected social roles.

rationales.benevolence: She stayed for "forty minutes" to help Diego write to his father and reflects that many kids "just need someone to stop and listen," showing concrete care for others' needs.

rationales.self_direction: He chose to spend an extra hour "scaffolding the API layer" for Pieter's tool and contrasts that with a workday where decisions were "already been made," signaling preference for work with more personal agency.
rationales.stimulation: He says he felt "more engaged in that hour than I have all sprint" and contrasts it with repetitive PRs, Slack threads, and planning, showing a pull toward more interesting and energizing work.
